column1: # environment: Windows desktop, Compose-first Android UI, Mapbox token deferred by user

column7: homepage still lacks a draggable bottom sheet and the map stack is still Baidu-specific
Homepage switched from a fixed bottom panel to a draggable bottom-sheet scaffold, preparing the app for the Mapbox migration and a more natural map-first interaction model.
Bottom-sheet iteration failed during Gradle configuration because BuildConfig fields were added for future Mapbox token support without enabling buildFeatures.buildConfig.
BuildConfig support enabled and the homepage now uses a draggable bottom-sheet scaffold, keeping the app structurally ready for Mapbox token wiring in the next pass.
Homepage bottom-sheet iteration still failed verification because MainComposeScreen retained the unresolved MyLocation vector icon reference.

Homepage now uses a draggable bottom-sheet scaffold and the project is structurally prepared for deferred Mapbox token wiring; this pass changes interaction structure rather than reducing the polish gap yet.
Introduced a map provider abstraction and stopped Activities from constructing Baidu MapView directly, reducing the coupling before the actual Mapbox swap.
Map provider abstraction pass failed verification because MainActivity still typed dashboardMapView as MapView after the direct import cleanup.
Map provider abstraction now compiles: Activities no longer construct Baidu MapView directly, which clears the first structural blocker before the actual Mapbox SDK swap.
Fixed the BuildConfig token wiring so deferred Mapbox token support is now structurally safe and no longer breaks Java compilation.
